verses.text: Elihu challenges Job's complaint that there is no profit in being cleansed from sin. He takes up a careless saying. When suffering tempts us to think godliness pointless, that thought must be answered, not nursed.

Elihu challenges Job's complaint that there is no profit in being cleansed from sin. He takes up a careless saying. When suffering tempts us to think godliness pointless, that thought must be answered, not nursed.

Elihu challenges Job's complaint that there is no profit in being cleansed from sin. He takes up a careless saying. When suffering tempts us to think godliness pointless, that thought must be answered, not nursed.

Look up to the heavens, says Elihu: your sin cannot harm the exalted God, nor your goodness enrich Him; they touch only your fellow men. God is above our gain or loss. We serve Him for His worth, not because He needs us.

Look up to the heavens, says Elihu: your sin cannot harm the exalted God, nor your goodness enrich Him; they touch only your fellow men. God is above our gain or loss. We serve Him for His worth, not because He needs us.

The oppressed cry under their burden, but few ask, 'where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night?' They cry from pain, not from faith. God can give His people songs even in their darkest hours, if they will seek Him.

The oppressed cry under their burden, but few ask, 'where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night?' They cry from pain, not from faith. God can give His people songs even in their darkest hours, if they will seek Him.

The oppressed cry under their burden, but few ask, 'where is God my maker, who giveth songs in the night?' They cry from pain, not from faith. God can give His people songs even in their darkest hours, if they will seek Him.
They cry and are not answered, because of the pride of evil men; God will not hear mere vanity. Empty, proud crying gets no reply. The prayer God answers rises from a humble, seeking heart.
They cry and are not answered, because of the pride of evil men; God will not hear mere vanity. Empty, proud crying gets no reply. The prayer God answers rises from a humble, seeking heart.

Though Job says he cannot see God, judgment is before Him; let Job wait and trust. Elihu charges him with multiplying empty words. Faith trusts the unseen God and stills its complaints in patient waiting.
